title: Prosthesis discomfort documentation

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-documented mobility, activities of daily living, rehabilitation goals, self-directed or supervised rehabilitation, functional impairment, and physiotherapy or occupational-therapy follow-up.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports postoperative rehabilitation context for joint-prosthesis discomfort; no decision is generated.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented mobility, activities of daily living, rehabilitation goals, self-directed or supervised rehabilitation, functional impairment, and physiotherapy or occupational-therapy follow-up.

source.title: Joint replacement (primary): hip, knee and shoulder — Recommendations

referral.text: Supports orthopaedic review for new or worsening pain, limp, or loss of function related to a joint replacement while noting the absence of a general fixed monitoring schedule.
referral.rationale: Supports long-term care and referral context for joint-prosthesis discomfort; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports orthopaedic review for new or worsening pain, limp, or loss of function related to a joint replacement while noting the absence of a general fixed monitoring schedule.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Joint, prosthesis, operation date, pain pattern, function, wound, fever, instability, trauma, imaging and prior surgical review are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for joint-prosthesis discomfort.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
